content: This was a fantastic app and superior alternative to most writing docs until the AI rebrand. And, sure, everything has an AI feature nowadays– that which you don't have to use if you don't wish to. But Notion in particular seems insistent on shoving it down their users throats (for lack of a better term)... Basically, it's far too annoying to put up with. And with no obligation to, I will be searching for a better place to keep my work.

content: Notion helps me manage every detail of my personal and professional life. It has simplified the process of tracking & accessing everything from my recipes and shopping lists to working on team projects with colleagues. Notion also saved me a lot of money & time as I had previously slugged along with disjointed tools. As I discovered the power of Notion, I was able to cancel subscriptions to Trello, Todoist, Evernote, & other apps. It's a game-changer. Not sure how I ever lived without it!

content: The many free templates are a godsend. I use Assignment Tracker and absolutely love it. It's a bit much to learn but over time, you'll become familiar with how to make your own templates. It has really helped me keep track with college. Between this app and 'Structured' (seperate app), it's been so easy to plan when my assignments are due, how urgent, and when I want to work on them. Again, it's a bit confusing to learn and manipulate, but the templates are the real money shots here.

content: It's actually a decent note taking app when you are connected to the Internet. But it's is worse than worthless when you are out of cell service and offline like when out of town, or on an airplane, or in the driveway and the Wi-Fi is too weak to work but the phone won't switch to 5G... The app closes repeatedly but will sort-of work for a minute or two and then close and lose your note. I lost a bunch of stuff and it seems that the whole folder is corrupted now... It's a mess.

content: On desktop Notion is a godsend, but getting anything done in this app is like pulling teeth while on fire it's so finicky and difficult to find what you're looking for. Plus if you leave the app for more than a minute, it puts you back at the homepage, which gets annoying fast if you have long or in-depth pages. Notion is a great program, just not on mobile. Also they need to add a toggle to disable AI integration so bad I'm so tired of seeing it. I straight up just do not care about it.

content: Notion's databases are amazing, and I love that you can see the data in multiple ways. Awesome. But, I don't care for the way the handle backlinks - kind of a lazy developer way to do it - and the icon for linked pages is obnoxious, ruining for me an otherwise pretty UI. The biggest thing is that you cannot store the data offline, locally. I work for a Fortune 500 company that doesn't allow info to be stored online by a 3rd party. Users, companies want to own their data. Bad.

content: Finding out that Notion didn't have an offline mode was an unpleasant surprise for me. I needed to make an important note, but didn't have reception or WiFi. Ended up just writing in Obsidian. On that note (ha), no other note taking app is missing this critical feature. I started to move my notes to Notion, but now will be moving back away until it has a stable offline mode. It's just not acceptable, especially if you're charging money.

content: A fine, basic list-making app. It updates quickly and accurately between platforms, which was the main function I was looking for. Still, it's my go-to list app, and that says something!

content: Nifty service for note taking, and so much more. I love that the free membership allows for some limited sharing, which is a great way when you want to share grocery lists with a spouse or party plans with a friend, without needing everyone to have a paid account (I currently have a paid account through work). content: Notion seems to be much, much more oriented to desktop users, ones who always have a steady Internet connection and can load things quickly. I primarily use Notion on my phone, on normally slow data or WiFi connections, which can make loading the app and trying to quickly save notes a hassle, especially if they don't upload before you close the app and they're lost! Unfortunately, I'm going to be switching to Google Keep. I love the complexity Notion allows in notes, though.

When I open the page on my phone, I expect it to sync as quickly as my Google Keep notes does, buy when I get to the store, I have to open and close the app several times, and sometimes it just doesn't sync at all. I also can never expect Notion to open offline.

content: My favorite note-taking app. It has tons of templates and is really easy to organize things. Adding pictures is super easy too but I wish that we had more freedom when moving and sizing the pictures. The sharing feature is awesome and very smart. It allowed you to share any page and share notes, while also allowing whoever you shared it with to add their own notes. There are so many uses beyond note taking in this app, like scheduling, to-do lists, etc.

I mostly find it's difficult to put information in while typing on this notion app, but if you use voice to text it works better.

content: Way too much work and upkeep for it to EVER be productive. Don't fall for the influencer hype. When a note taking app's feature which sets it apart from all the others requires watching videos and spending 30+ min to setup for each subject, its literally the opposite of the definition of productive. You will set up something cute. You'll use it for a day or two. You'll never go back to it because it's way too complicated when you just want to write a quick note and a reminder to study notes.

content: Notion is an amazing, versatile tool. I love how you can start with a simple page, make a list, add a database, add a graphical bookmark and so much more. It just flows with me as a thought or task unfolds. Then as I develop that thought, it can easily transform into a different part that makes sense with where I'm at in that flow. Cross platform support is really good and consistent. The Notion team is always working to enhance the app and extend its functionality. They really listen well too.

content: I pray for Evernote to last for the rest of my life. Notion does way more than I need day to day, and it runs like it. Too long to open, too hard to navigate and, after 3 months trying it out, I still feel like I have a loose stack of handwritten notes crammed into my phone. The last straw was not being able to figure out how to zoom in on a picture I put on a page. I tried dragging fingers apart, double tapping and looking for "zoom in" under ... , then gave up. Only if I have to, Notion.

content: Incredible! Best note-taking app I've used so far. They even have dark mode! Very satisfied.

content: This used to be my favorite notes/to-do list apps, but not anymore. When I first got this app, it would load right away and I could easily scroll through my different lists. If I had no WiFi, I could simply add something to my list and it would be synced as soon as I connected again. As of late, however, this app takes several seconds longer to load, crashes an average of three times before I can open it once, and has syncing issues. It's a shame to see such a great app fall apart like this.

content: In the few months I've been using Notion, it has quickly become one of my favorite apps ever. This does what I always wished OneNote and Ever Note and Keep did: it allows me to organize my workspace the way it makes sense to me, using a combination of pages and blocks and wikis and lists and boards, and have different work spaces for the different things I have going on. Additionally, the integration is well thought-out, and the support staff are friendly and helpful. Highly recommended.

content: One thing that I had an issue with on most note-keeping apps is syncing. Dropbox Paper and several other apps where I would write things would not save properly, but Notion syncs everything so immediately and seamlessly, even if it shows you're offline. On top of that, it's free and no annoying ads. That's unheard of these days, although I don't mind paying a little because it's such an amazing app. Have everything organized well now.

content: Personal user. So far so good. Notion is helping me journal better, and more often. Their layout is simple and easy to figure out, but also very customizable. I use the android app 99% of the time. I just wish the dev's would fix cursor jumping, because it happens allot, at least once every time I'm journaling a new entry. I have had zero issues with the app not properly saving my content (unlike Microsoft Notes) If that's the reason for cursor jump I'll take the trade-off.

content: I love using Notion as an all around productivity tool. It's the most versatile I've ever used. The Android app however, leaves a lot to be desired at the moment. Tables have been unreliable for me on the Android app. Multiple times per day, all my table entries disappear, or my calculations at the bottom of columns disappear. There's no true offline support either. Unfortunately, these issues make the Android app difficult to depend on, and I've switched to the webapp.

content: I use Notion for everything. Literally. From work (task management, documentations) to personal shopping lists, to-dos, research, meal planner, recipes, etc. Their customer support is one of the best I've met so far, very responsive (didn't expect to get answers so fast, even if I was on a free plan at that time), very friendly and solution-oriented (no copy-paste). The tool itself is super flexible and well designed.

content: This used to be my favorite, for notes and personal information management. It is extremely effective for creating wikis. Both internal and external hyperlinks are easy to create and use. The app has virtually all of the functionality that the web and Windows versions do, although tables are somewhat easier to use on desktop. Databases/tables have a bit of a learning curve, but are very useful. Very feature rich app. Can be overwhelming, and the rigid organizational structure is not ideal.

content: Notion has definitely helped me with my productivity. I used it for daily task, long-term study planning (from 1-6th semester of my uni), and for working and organizational purposes. Too bad that not many people use Notion. It'll take time for a beginner to navigate through the UI, but definitely worth using after you learn how to automate your task and synchronize it with the Google Calendar. P.S if you have ADHD: Don't over engineer your notion, keep it simple.

content: I love the new AI Meeting Notes, but there is a major issue for international users. When I speak Greek, I can see the AI transcribing it perfectly in Greek for a few seconds. However, because Greek isn't "officially" in the settings, Notion then auto-translates it into English. Please add a "Preserve Original Language" toggle! The engine clearly supports more languages than the settings allow. If we could just stop the auto-translation, this would be a 5-star tool for everyone!

content: What makes Notion especially strong is that it connects thinking and execution in the same place. The feature I value most is how notes and tasks live together, because good planning usually depends on turning ideas into action without losing context. That sounds simple, but very few tools handle it well. I appreciate software that helps people stay organized without fragmenting their attention, and Notion does that with unusual flexibility and depth.

content: Astounding experience: good UI. However there's a little bit in the UX I want to point out: Selecting/blocking multiple blocks feels janky, how about hold to select multiple blocks instead? content: It's been years since many bugs were reported, and nothing changes. Makes me consider other systems. Painfully annoying that the only way to load white pages (i.e. pages with content that are not loading for some reason, blank screen) is to close and open the app again, and then to load the page. Sometimes it can be dozens of times a day. Sometimes it can ruin your work, since closing the app in the middle of the process is not always a good idea. But that's the only option. Really, Notion?

content: Glitchy and hard to correct. This had been recommended to me for Project Management. I've used a variety of tools for organisation and planning before, so always open to trying a new format. It glitches when you try to give certain commands so you then need to go to the main tab and click 'Undo' repeatedly just to get back to where you were.

content: Notion is not just a productivity app — it’s a system for thinking clearly. I’ve tried almost every popular tool: Google Docs, Trello, Evernote, OneNote, Asana — and all of them feel limited once you use Notion properly. Those apps force you into predefined workflows. Notion does the opposite: it adapts to how you think and work. What makes Notion powerful is how everything connects. Notes, tasks, projects, calendars, goals, knowledge — all live in one place, fully linked, searchable, and custom

content: Update: They've gone all in on AI and I no longer feel comfortable keeping my notes in this app, and as such I've moved away from Notion. Since all data is stored in the cloud, we've also been instructed not to use Notion for work notes out of security concerns. I've since moved to Obsidian, which only operates locally (unless you explicitly sync to cloud yourself). A shame, since Notion on it's own is quite frankly a fantastic app.

content: I use it on a daily basis. On the PC too, its extremely handy! But it does have its problems. It doesn't let you type then save when it can, its entirely networked based. content: Amazing, well designed app (not only the Android version, desktop and web app as well). content: Notion is a perfect app for me to take my notes digitally. It looks so aesthetic and pretty when I used the app as a beginner. However, whenever I spent a long time to organize my notes, it can get really slow and laggy sometimes. This apparently annoys me the most. Other than that, I am also dissapointed that I can't save my notes locally and always have to rely on the internet to save my notes. I do not have online connection all the times, ya know.

Although the only good thing about Android version is, it lets me upload video clips of any size I want, whereas the PC version has a limit of 5MB.

content: I've been using notion for about half a year now for many different things - like note-taking, planning rpg campaigns and tracking personal stuff - and to be honest it's one of the best apps I've ever used. It may be a little confusing at first, but once you get the hang of it It's amazing. The only thing I find disappointing is that the mobile app lacks quite a few features, like making inline equations, changing gallery's card sized, resizing embedded images and embedding pdfs.

content: I loved Notion while I gave it a try, mainly for college note-taking, and I found so much more. It allows one to organize other things in formats other than simple notes. However, because of that, its note-taking features are not as stand-out as other options. Its search is not as good, and its offline features still have a ways to go (for example, I can't use the custom page copy button offline). The final thing is that the mobile app is slow to the point of being unusable, sadly.

content: Haven't used it for a while and have just come back to it and the Android and Windows apps are slow (to type or select text) and buggy. Don't know what they've done wrong with it, perhaps it's all the AI nonsense. Note apps should be fast and zippy. They should sync in the background (even when not in use) and the mobile or desktop app should NEVER log you out unless you explicitly log yourself out. More effort required on the basics.

content: This is definitely the best all in one workspace app. The templates are very useful but there's also so much freedom and versatility to create your own pages the way you want. I highly recommend to any student, professional and anyone else who needs to organise their work. My only major problem is how slow the application is both on mobile and desktop. It can take a long time to load up and switch pages which is a shame when you need to take quick notes. I wish it was more lightweight.

content: I love Notion and what it can mean for me. The mobile app just isn't up to scratch. I want to be able to edit tables like moving columns or making them wider or smaller. Also I'm unable to link to an existing page. It pops up pages I can link and then instantly disappears before I can actually set it up. Editing my notion on mobile is an absolute HASSLE. Not great if I want to use it as a daily journal for on the go. I am forced to carry a laptop with me just to do basic editing.

content: I absolutely love Notion on the desktop, it's wonderful for anything from simple todo lists to larger projects to just regular notetaking. But, this app is just way way way too slow. Loading takes forever, and once it does load, everything is just so choppy. I can barely drag a block of text. Add to that the fact that a lot of simple keyboard shortcuts from desktop just don't exist in a quickly accessible way, and you can see why you basically can't be productive with it on mobile.

content: This app is exceptional, although a little slow on the phone but still manageable. I'd suggest some things to add/improve. I wish we are able to read pdfs w/o saving it to our device first like in the laptop version. Next, I wish there was a way to input a handwritten notes, doesn't have to be a full fledged feature like OneNote, just the ability to sketch diagrams/notes and input it alongside typed notes would be massive change. Keep it up!

content: The app itself is one of the greatest notetaking apps I have ever come across. However, the writing experience on the mobile app is incredibly awful. At times when the internet connection is a tiny bit weak, the words refuse to appear on the page and I'm forced to type out three times slower now usually type. This is kind of detrimental since ideas come and go quite quickly so unless I'm able to write it down immediately, little epiphanies, unique thoughts, perspectives get lost all the time.

content: This is an extremely customizable note taking app. You can use it pretty much for everything: quick notes, journaling, to do tasks and so much more. It also has necessary clients for other platforms. Now that there are no more block limits in its free version, it is recommended even more to everyone. But be aware of the learning curve and getting used to it. It has so much going on that I was feeling lost at first everytime I opened the app.

content: It took me a little time to get used to it, but I love Notion. It allows me to format data in so many ways which is a great help to a visual person like me. Firstly, there is some issue with the typing for a few days now where some letters get double typed. Secondly, the app doesn't load at all now and keeps giving me an error message. I really hope my notes aren't gone :(

content: The Android version is dead slow and sometimes the app doesn't even load. No widgets too. Having said this, I like the options that it gives to write notes. content: Unfortunately I'm going to have to update my review: I've been using Notion now for over a year and its unfortunately getting worse. I liked notion because it was unified across all my devices. But typing in the android app is near impossible now. Updates aren't saved, it's slow and unresponsive, and it randomly crashes out. As someone trying to do a lot of writing, I'm afraid it's not longer suitable. Il likely have to switch to a more reliable provider now, and give Obsidian a try.

content: I love Notion - I have my whole life in it. But the mobile app alone is not very usable. It's clunky and UI could be much better. It lacks some options from desktop. Download this only if you intend on mainly using the desktop version, and having mobile app as a way of quickly checking stuff you added on desktop. Also it's not good for quick note taking so if thats what you looking for, skip it.

content: I seriously like Notion; it's such a cool workspace, but all the issues I've been having are just god damn annoying. While I'm writing a paragraph, my cursor will randomly jump upwards into a different paragraph and that not just once but constantly. Lagging is also still an issue. This has been going on for months now, and it's seriously annoying. I've talked to a friend who uses Notion as well; however, they do not have these issues. They are an iOS user while I am an Andriod user. It's sad.
